entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: The crop studied, Arachis hypogaea L., evaluated for water-use efficiency under drought conditions.

entities.name: WATER-USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency of water use (W), measured as g kg−1, which varies among genotypes.

entities.name: CARBON ISOTOPE DISCRIMINATION
entities.description: The discrimination of carbon isotopes in leaves (Δ), used as a proxy for identifying genotypes with greater water-use efficiency.

entities.name: WATER DEFICIT
entities.description: Drought regimes, including intermittent and prolonged water deficit conditions.

entities.name: MINI-LYSIMETERS
entities.description: Tools embedded in soil used for accurate measurement of water use and total dry matter in a canopy environment.

entities.name: TOTAL DRY MATTER
entities.description: The total dry mass of the plant, including roots, which influences water-use efficiency.

entities.name: TIFTON-8
entities.description: A peanut genotype that exhibited the highest water-use efficiency.

entities.name: CHICO
entities.description: A peanut genotype that exhibited the lowest water-use efficiency.

entities.name: SPECIFIC LEAF AREA
entities.description: SLA, a measure of leaf thickness that is negatively correlated with water-use efficiency.

entities.name: MASS SPECTROMETRY
entities.description: Analytical technique used to measure carbon isotope discrimination.

entities.name: RAIN-OUT SHELTERS
entities.description: Automated shelters used to prevent rain from reaching experimental plots to maintain drought conditions.

relationships.description: Negatively correlated, making carbon isotope discrimination a useful trait for selecting genotypes with improved water-use efficiency
The crop in which water-use efficiency was measured
The crop in which carbon isotope discrimination was measured
The crop subjected to intermittent and prolonged water deficit conditions
Trait evaluated under drought conditions in the field
Used to accurately measure water use for calculating water-use efficiency
Used to accurately measure total dry matter including roots
Variation in water-use efficiency arose mainly from genotypic differences in total dry matter production
Genotype with the highest recorded water-use efficiency
Genotype with the lowest recorded water-use efficiency
Strong negative relationship where genotypes with thicker leaves had greater water-use efficiency
Relationship exists between carbon isotope discrimination and specific leaf area
Facility/method used to measure carbon isotope discrimination
Used to prevent rain from reaching plots to ensure water deficit conditions
